title: The Betwa River Board Act, 1976
full_text: The Betwa River Board Act, 1976 बेतवा नदी बोर्ड अधिनियम, 1976 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 197663 Act Number: 63 Enactment Date: 1976-04-10 Act Year: 1976 Short Title: The Betwa River Board Act, 1976 Hindi Title: बेतवा नदी बोर्ड अधिनियम, 1976 Long Title: An Act to provide for the establishment of a Board for the creation of a reservoir at Rajghat by construction, on behalf of the Governments of Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh, of a dam on the Betwa river at Rajghat and for the regulation of such reservoir. Ministry: Ministry of Jal Shaktri Department: Department of Water Resources, River Development and Ganga Rejuvenation Enforcement Date: 25-10-1976 Notification: 25th October, 1976, vide notification No. G.S.R. 1518(E), dated 14th October, 1976, see Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, sec. 3(i). Schedule 1. THE SCHEDULE Order Appendix Forms TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles Show all section The Betwa River Board Act, 1976 CHAPTER I PRELIMINARY CHAPTER II ESTABLISHMENT OF THE BOARD CHAPTER III FUNCTIONS AND POWERS OF THE BOARD CHAPTER IV FINANCE, ACCOUNTS AND AUDIT CHAPTER V MISCELLANEOUS Section 1. Short title and commencement. Section 2. Declaration as to expediency of control by the Union. Section 3. Definitions. Section 4. Establishment and incorporation of the Betwa River Board. Section 5. Executive Committee. Section 6. Vacancies, etc., not to invalidate proceedings of the Board or the Executive Committee. Section 7. Chief Engineer and Financial Adviser. Section 8. Other officers and employees of the Board. Section 9. Advisory Committees. Section 10. Functions of the Board. Section 11. Conditions subject to which the Board may exercise its functions. Section 12. Powers of the Board. Section 13. Betwa River Board Fund. Section 14. Budget. Section 15. Annual report. Section 16. Accounts and audit. Section 17. Directions by Central Government. Section 18. Disputes between the Board and the State Governments. Section 19. Power to enter. Section 20. Members, officers and employees of the Board to be public servants. Section 21. Protection of action taken in good faith. Section 22. Power to make rules. Show Related Subordinates Section 23. Power to make regulations. Show Related Subordinates Section 24. Rules and regulations to be laid before Parliament. Schedule 1.
